perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, "Confidential Informat ion" refers to any information which entails personal details of fello w members, supporters, associates such as names, ad dresses, telephone numbers and other contact details as well as operational manuals, methods, computer software , works of art, drafts and designs, financial data, p laces of meetings, secrets and other proprietary in formation related to the past, current, future and proposed a ctivities of the Society, and any other informatio n which is privileged, proprietary and confidential.
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, "Confidential Informat ion" refers to information related to the activities of the Society. This includes financial data and operational manuals.
perturbation.explanation: By significantly narrowing the definition of 'Confidential Information' in Section 1, the scope of what is protected is reduced. The original text lists names, address, telephone numbers but these were removed and are no longer considered confidential, which is in direct conflict with the Nondisclosure and Nonuse Obligations in Section 2, especially considering the original intent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient will maintain in confidence and will no t disclose, disseminate or use any Confidential Infor mation belonging to and/or related to Society, whet her or not in written form. Recipient agrees that Recipient s hall treat all Confidential Information of the Soci ety with at least the same degree of care as Recipient accords its own confidential information. Recipient furthe r represents that Recipient exercises at least reason able care to protect its own confidential informati on.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' by removing specific examples like names, addresses, and telephone numbers of members, supporters, and associates. The contradicted text imposes a nondisclosure and nonuse obligation on the Recipient regarding 'any Confidential Information belonging to and/or related to Society'. Because the definition of Confidential Information was significantly narrowed in the Changed Text, this is a direct conflict, as information originally intended to be confidential is no longer covered under the new definition, therefore creating a strong in-text contradiction. This adheres to the definition of Omissions- In Text Contradiction because essential language was removed creating a contradiction with obligations in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient will maintain in confidence and will no t disclose, disseminate or use any Confidential Infor mation belonging to and/or related to Society, whet her or not in written form. Recipient agrees that Recipient s hall treat all Confidential Information of the Soci ety with at least the same degree of care as Recipient accords its own confidential information. Recipient furthe r represents that Recipient exercises at least reason able care to protect its own confidential informati on.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will endeavor to maintain in confidence and will no t disclose, disseminate or use any Confidential Information belonging to and/or related to Society, whet her or not in written form. Recipient agrees that Recipient shall treat all Confidential Information of the Soci ety with reasonable care. Recipient represents that Recipient exercises reasonable care to protect its own confidential information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "maintain in confidence" to "endeavor to maintain in confidence" and removing "at least the same degree of care as Recipient accords its own confidential information" and replacing it with "reasonable care", this makes the obligation significantly weaker. This weakens the effect of section 5 "Injunctive Relief" since the requirement has become harder to prove in court.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A breach of any of the promises or agreements co ntained herein will result in irreparable and continuing damage to the Society fo r which there will be no adequate remedy at law, an d the Society shall be entitled to injunctive relief and/ or a decree for specific performance, and such othe r relief as may be proper (including monetary damages if approp riate).
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the obligation of the recipient regarding confidential information. The original text states "Recipient will maintain in confidence" and "Recipient shall treat all Confidential Information of the Society with at least the same degree of care as Recipient accords its own confidential information", while the changed text uses "Recipient will endeavor to maintain in confidence" and "Recipient shall treat all Confidential Information of the Society with reasonable care". This change introduces a weaker standard of care. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states that "A breach of any of the promises or agreements contained herein will result in irreparable and continuing damage to the Society fo r which there will be no adequate remedy at law, an d the Society shall be entitled to injunctive relief". Because the standard of care has been lowered and makes a breach harder to prove, the right to injunctive relief is now less certain, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall govern all communications b etween the parties. Recipient understands that its obligations under Paragraph 2 ("Nondisclosure and Nonuse Obligations") shall surv ive the termination of any other relationship between the p arties. Upon termination of any relationship betwe en the parties, Recipient will promptly deliver to the Soc iety, without retaining any copies, all documents a nd other materials furnished to Recipient by the Society.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall govern all communications b etween the parties. Recipient understands that its obligations under Paragraph 2 ("Nondisclosure and Nonuse Obligations") shall surv ive the termination of any other relationship between the p arties. Upon termination of any relationship betwe en the parties, Recipient will deliver to the Soc iety, all documents a nd other materials furnished to Recipient by the Society.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "without retaining any copies" it now allows the recipient to retain copies of the confidential data. This creates a contradiction, because it allows the recipient to retain the data, but still agrees to the non-disclosure and non-use obligations described in section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient will maintain in confidence and will no t disclose, disseminate or use any Confidential Infor mation belonging to and/or related to Society, whet her or not in written form. Recipient agrees that Recipient s hall treat all Confidential Information of the Soci ety with at least the same degree of care as Recipient accords its own confidential information. Recipient furthe r represents that Recipient exercises at least reason able care to protect its own confidential informati on.
perturbation.justification: The original text required the recipient to return all documents and materials "without retaining any copies." The changed text removes this restriction, now permitting the recipient to retain copies. This directly contradicts the section requiring the recipient to maintain confidentiality and not disclose or use confidential information. Retaining copies of confidential information inherently increases the risk of disclosure and use, creating a contradiction between the changed text and the contradicted text, since the changed text allows the recipient to have copies, while the contradicted text mandates non-disclosure of the confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed in all respects by the laws of the Commonwealth of Australia and by the laws of the St ate of Victoria; as such laws are applied to agreem ents entered into and to be performed entirely within Au stralia.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the St ate of Victoria; as such laws are applied to agreem ents entered into and to be performed entirely within Au stralia.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "in all respects by the laws of the Commonwealth of Australia" this will weaken the effect of the entire agreement. This contradicts Section 5, Injunctive Relief, because a breach under Commonwealth Law will not be considered anymore.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A breach of any of the promises or agreements co ntained herein will result in irreparable and continuing damage to the Society fo r which there will be no adequate remedy at law, an d the Society shall be entitled to injunctive relief and/ or a decree for specific performance, and such othe r relief as may be proper (including monetary damages if approp riate).
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement was governed by both the laws of the Commonwealth of Australia and the laws of the State of Victoria. The changed text removes the reference to the laws of the Commonwealth of Australia. The contradicted text states that a breach of 'any of the promises or agreements contained herein' will result in irreparable damage. By removing the reference to Commonwealth Law, a breach under Commonwealth Law is no longer considered a breach of the agreement, thus creating a contradiction with the section about breaches, as described in the explanation.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement with respect to the Confidential Information disclosed herein and super sedes all prior or contemporaneous oral or written agreements concerning such Confidential Information . This Agreement may only be changed by mutual agreement between the parties in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the agreement with respect to the Confidential Information disclosed herein and super sedes all prior oral agreements concerning such Confidential Information . This Agreement may be changed by mutual agreement between the parties.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "entire" "or contemporaneous" and "in writing" it introduces ambiguity to the enforcement of the entire agreement. Removing 'entire' opens the possibility that other agreements exist outside of this one. Removing 'or contemporaneous' opens the door for claims based on agreements made at the same time but not included. Finally, removing 'in writing' contradicts the purpose of a written contract and creates uncertainty because it is not clear how the contract can be changed, or what kind of change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the agreement with respect to the Confidential Information disclosed herein and super sedes all prior oral agreements concerning such Confidential Information . This Agreement may be changed by mutual agreement between the parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'entire', 'or contemporaneous', and 'in writing'. According to the Perturbation Explanation, this introduces ambiguity and undermines the enforceability of the agreement by opening the door for claims based on prior or contemporaneous oral agreements. The removal of 'in writing' creates further uncertainty about how the contract can be changed. Since the changed text and contradicted text are the same, the changes introduce contradictions, as specified by the prompt.
